_**Agent (auto-low)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The full picture is now clear.

Six files need updates. The bible shortcode partial and notes renderer regex must drop the parentheses and trailing period. Three Python scripts also need regex adjustments.

Documentation and all note markdown files need bulk updates too. Dropping parentheses creates ambiguity, since verse text may contain parenthetical phrases. Checking mercy.md to see how the reference is currently separated.

The reference will sit directly after parenthetical verse text. The renderer regex must match a reference ending in ` Book Ref NASB1995` without parentheses or period.

Drafting the renderer regex. It will match a paragraph containing the reference at its end. A simple greedy pattern risks capturing too much text. A stricter pattern using book names and verse numbers is preferable.

The renderer regex will match from the end of the paragraph, anchored by `NASB1995` without a trailing period. It uses an explicit list of book names and verse numbers.

The same change applies to the gospel jesus-words patterns.

In `normalize-verse-paragraphs.py`, the `verse_line` template drops the parentheses and period. The `PAREN_GROUP` regex and `refs_on_line` logic must be updated to recognize references in the new format.
</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The goal is clear. I will remove the parenthesis from the verse reference.

Seven files need updates. The regex in render-content.html must match the new format. Parenthetical text inside verses requires careful handling.

The regex pattern targets specific books including Matthew, Mark, Luke, John, Romans, Ephesians, Galatians, Deuteronomy, Corinthians, James, Hebrews, Psalm, Psalms, and Proverbs. It matches the verse reference ending with NASB1995.

Three regex patterns are needed. One handles plain paragraphs with NASB references at the end. Another handles gospel references still trapped inside scripture-text spans. A third handles scripture-verse blocks that lack the current span split. The replacement template wraps the verse text in a scripture-text span and the reference in a scripture-ref span.

The bible.html shortcode already emits the correct HTML spans, so the regex in render-content.html only needs to handle raw markdown paragraphs. For normalize-verse-paragraphs.py, the verse line format changes to append the canonical reference directly after the text. A new trailing-reference regex pattern is required for references at the end of lines. The existing inline parenthetical regex will remain for legacy inline citations. A Python BOOK regex is being defined to match the target books.

Two regex patterns are now defined. One captures parenthetical references containing a book name. The other matches trailing references ending with NASB1995. A new `strip_refs` function will remove both types of references from verse lines. Deduplication will check for the NASB1995 suffix at the end of each line.

The regex in apply-verse-explanations.py now matches trailing references ending with NASB1995 instead of parenthetical ones. The emit_verse_block function appends a period if the verse text does not already end with one.
</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Updating `apply-verse-explanations.py` to remove the parenthetical verse reference format.

Adjusting regex patterns and verse block logic to handle references without parentheses. Inline extraction now uses `m.group(1)` directly. The period is omitted when a verse ends with "NASB1995".

Refining the regex to capture the full reference including NASB1995 via a named group. The inline-after-reference pattern must now match a trailing reference followed by a space and the remainder of the line.

The `split_verse_chunks` function will use the named `ref` group to extract the reference. A second file, `apply-jesus-words-shortcode.py`, also needs its Gospel regex updated to match the new reference format.

The Gospel regex in `apply-jesus-words-shortcode.py` now captures verse ranges such as `7:7-11` or `6:9-13` before `NASB1995`. A bulk `sed` command was drafted to strip the old parenthetical wrapper from existing notes.

The bulk sed replacement correctly targets only the NASB1995 suffix, leaving inner parenthetical phrases intact. Verses ending with a period before the reference will now have that period removed. Lines without a trailing period before the reference remain unchanged.
</details></think>

Implementing the requested change now.

Planning regex replacements for render-content.html. Each file keeps its own book list. Pattern 1 converts plain paragraphs containing the verse reference into scripture-verse spans.

Adding regex patterns to colorize gospel references. Matthew, Mark, Luke, and John will get a jesus-words class. Other books get default styling. The gospel pattern must run first to avoid conflicts.

Simplifying the regex strategy to two patterns: gospel references and other books. bible.html shortcode output will be handled directly without post-processing. Legacy partial wraps will be updated to the new format.

The migration ensures all markdown uses the new format, so only three regex passes are needed: plain gospel paragraphs, plain other-book paragraphs, and legacy partial wraps. Shortcode output is already correct and needs no post-processing.

Updating normalize-verse-paragraphs.py. The Python script will use a REF_SUFFIX regex to catch trailing references outside parentheses. The function strip_paren_refs is being renamed to strip_refs.

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="run_terminal_command_v2"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: cd /home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com &amp;&amp; python3 -c &#34;
import re
from pathlib import Path
pat = re.compile(r&#39;\(([^)]+ NASB1995)\)\.&#39;)
notes = Path(&#39;content/english/notes&#39;)
changed = 0
for path in sorted(notes.glob(&#39;*.md&#39;)):
    text = path.read_text(encoding=&#39;utf-8&#39;)
    new = pat.sub(r&#39;\1&#39;, text)
    if new != text:
        path.write_text(new, encoding=&#39;utf-8&#39;)
        changed += 1
        print(path.name)
print(f&#39;Done — {changed} note(s)&#39;)
&#34;
</summary>
```bash
cd /home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com && python3 -c "
import re
from pathlib import Path
pat = re.compile(r'\(([^)]+ NASB1995)\)\.')
notes = Path('content/english/notes')
changed = 0
for path in sorted(notes.glob('*.md')):
    text = path.read_text(encoding='utf-8')
    new = pat.sub(r'\1', text)
    if new != text:
        path.write_text(new, encoding='utf-8')
        changed += 1
        print(path.name)
print(f'Done — {changed} note(s)')
"

```

```
dont-copy-the-pattern.md
free-grace.md
fruits-of-the-spirit.md
grace.md
judgment-seat.md
justification.md
loss-of-reward.md
mercy.md
present-a-living-sacrifice.md
renewed-mind.md
Done — 10 note(s)

```
</details></tool-use>
